event: in the 26th week of her first pregnancy
rectum was empty on digital examination
foetal viability was assessed by an obstetrician
vaginal examination was not suggestive of threatened preterm labour
ultrasound scan of the abdomen and pelvis showed a distended bowel loop and a moderate amount of free fluid in the peritoneal cavity
moderate amount of free fluid in the peritoneal cavity
plain X-ray of the abdomen revealed a dilated large bowel with an abnormal gas pattern and a coffee bean appearance
sigmoidoscopy confirmed the diagnosis of a twisted sigmoid colon
not possible to negotiate the obstruction
decision was to perform a concomitant caesarean section
taken to the emergency theatre for laparotomy
necrosis resulted from the presence of SV
necrotic colon was posteriorly displaced by the pregnant uterus
performed a lower segment caesarean section
deliver a male preterm infant weighting 750 g
placed on mechanical ventilation due to lung immaturity
faced with the gangrenous sigmoid colon, resection was performed
end colostomy was fashioned with closure of the rectal stump below the peritoneal reflection
discharged home on the 9th post-operative day
child was discharged home following 10 weeks in the neonatal intensive care unit
underwent reversal of Hartmann’s, with bowel continuity restored through colo-rectal anastomosis
the authors were involved in the second procedure
